title: 10 best herbal capsules for post nasal drip

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain the herb Echinacea, which has been traditionally used to support immune system function and reduce inflammation. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate congestion and sinus pressure associated with post nasal drip, while also helping to boost the body's natural defenses against infection. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's antimicrobial properties may help combat bacterial or viral infections that can contribute to post nasal drip.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cichoric acid, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating post-nasal drip symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Kansas Coneflower capsules, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, help protect the mucous membranes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the severity of post-nasal drip.

Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower has been traditionally used to support immune function, which can help combat underlying infections or allergies that may be contributing to post-nasal drip, thereby alleviating symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides, particularly echinacoside, help reduce post nasal drip by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate nasal congestion and excess mucus production.

Polyphenolic compounds: Polyphenolic compounds, such as caffeic acid, ferulic acid, and sinapic acid, possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce the growth of pathogens that contribute to post nasal drip and soothe irritated nasal passages.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides, including cis- and trans-cadinene, have been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help stimulate the immune system and reduce the frequency and severity of post nasal drip episodes by preventing infections and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The underground stem system of the plant that contains the highest concentration of active compounds, including alkylamides and phenolic acids.
Roots: Roots: The root system of Echinacea angustifolia is rich in polysaccharides, which are believed to stimulate the immune system and help alleviate postnasal drip symptoms.

Roots: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots contain a range of bioactive compounds, including isobutylamides and echinacoside, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce postnasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest clean dry echinacea angustifolia roots at a ratio of 1:4 root to carrier material.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500mg of the powdered root material.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat and nasal passages. The licorice root extract found in these capsules has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and congestion, making it an effective remedy for relieving the symptoms of postnasal drip. By reducing swelling and irritation, herbal licorice capsules can help to alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of clearness and ease in the nasal passages.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.

Mucolytic properties: Herbal licorice capsules contain mucolytic agents that break down thick mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the sensation of a lump or cough associated with postnasal drip.

Expectorant properties: Licorice root has natural expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from congestion and coughing that often accompanies postnasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, which can contribute to post nasal drip.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages and reduce the occurrence of post nasal drip.

Flavonoids (specifically, liquiritin and isolariciresinol): Flavonoids like liquiritin and isolariciresinol have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce congestion and prevent the growth of pathogens that can contribute to post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a key compound that helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the nasal mucosa.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain saponins and other compounds that have expectorant properties, helping to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or cultivate them locally.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a mill.
Mix the powder with 200 grams of starch or cellulose and 50 grams of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Encapsulate the blend using a capsule machine or a manual capsule filler at a speed of 100 capsules per minute.
Label and date the capsules according to regulatory requirements and store them in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with post nasal drip because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages. The menthol in peppermint oil stimulates the mucous membranes, thinning out excess mucus and promoting drainage. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory effects reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal tissues, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion. By addressing these underlying causes of post nasal drip, peppermint capsules provide fast and effective relief from discomfort and congestion.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, a key constituent of peppermint oil, helps to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and postnasal drip.
Menthol: Menthol in peppermint has a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect, which can help relieve sinus pressure and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint capsules have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, including the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby reducing postnasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from post nasal drip by creating a decongesting and expectorant effect.
Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic compound): This compound has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and alleviate symptoms of post nasal drip.

Eugenol (a phenolic compound): Eugenol exhibits antimicrobial properties that help to combat infections in the nasal passages, reducing the production of excess mucus and providing relief from post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide a cooling and decongestant effect.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita contain a higher concentration of menthol than the leaves, making them a preferred choice for postnasal drip relief.
Roots: The roots of the plant have a higher concentration of menthone, which has expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh m x piperita leaves by cutting them with scissors when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 150g of dried m x piperita leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the powdered m x piperita with 1 tablespoon of gelatin powder and 2 tablespoons of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the m x piperita mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain the extract of Eucalyptus globulus, a natural decongestant that effectively relieves congestion and excess mucus in the nasal passages. The capsules' active ingredients work to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, thereby reducing the sensation of postnasal drip. Additionally, the eucalyptol present in the capsules has natural antibacterial properties that help combat underlying infections, further alleviating symptoms associated with postnasal drip.
remedy_properties: Mucolytic activity: Tasmanian Blue Gum's mucolytic properties help break down and thin excess mucus in the nasal passages, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion and postnasal drip.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in Tasmanian Blue Gum capsules have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can contribute to postnasal drip, helping to reduce inflammation and infection.

Expectorant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm and reducing the sensation of postnasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This monoterpene acts as a natural expectorant, helping to break down and expel mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, thereby relieving post-nasal drip.

Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have astringent properties that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, making it less likely for mucus to accumulate and cause post-nasal drip.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing the production of excess mucus and alleviating post-nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high oil content and medicinal properties, which help in relieving congestion and post nasal drip.
Barks: The bark of Eucalyptus globulus is also used, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in soothing and protecting the respiratory tract.
Stems: Although less commonly used than leaves, some products may use the stems of Eucalyptus globulus, possibly for their additional oil content and potential health benefits.
remedy_recipe: Dry freshly harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves in a single layer at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind 1 part of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 1 part of vegetable cellulose powder in a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground powder with a small amount of glycerin to facilitate capsule filling for better flow.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine with 500mg dosage.
Store the herbal eucalyptus globulus capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with post nasal drip because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause excessive mucus production, leading to post nasal drip. Additionally, thyme's decongestant and expectorant properties help to reduce nasal congestion and thin out mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving the constant sensation of a "runny nose" and coughing. This natural remedy provides relief from post nasal drip symptoms without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear excess mucus from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and postnasal drip.

Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antibacterial properties can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to postnasal drip, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy respiratory tract.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, providing relief from discomfort and congestion associated with postnasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol is a phenolic compound that has antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of pathogens in the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby alleviating post nasal drip.

Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide is a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, leading to a decrease in post nasal drip.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate is a terpene that has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to expel post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are used due to their high content of thymol, an antiseptic and expectorant compound that helps relieve post nasal drip.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used for their fragrance and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce congestion associated with post nasal drip.
Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are used due to their ability to act as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Dry the thymus vulgaris leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind 1 part dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250mg of the thymus vulgaris powder with 750mg of a filler material such as cornstarch.
Fill a capsule with the thymus vulgaris mixture using an encapsulation machine.
Store the herbal thymus vulgaris capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

Mix one part of the ground stevia powder with two parts of gelatin capsules filling material and fill capsules within an hour.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The capsules also increase mucus production, which can help to loosen and clear out excess mucus that is causing congestion and drip down the back of the throat. Additionally, fenugreek has expectorant properties, which can help to thin and expel mucus, providing relief from postnasal drip symptoms such as coughing and sinus pressure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fenugreek's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pressure associated with postnasal drip.

Antioxidant properties: Fenugreek's antioxidant properties help protect the mucous membranes from oxidative damage caused by environmental toxins and pollutants, which can contribute to postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and discomfort associated with post nasal drip.

Dry the cleaned seeds in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered trigonella foenum graecum with 500mg of vegetable glycerin and fill capsules according to the desired dosage.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier drainage and relief from congestion. Additionally, ginger's natural decongestant properties help to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the feeling of post nasal drip. Antihistamine-like property: Ginger's antihistamine-like effects can help block histamine receptors in the nasal passages, which can reduce excessive mucus production and alleviate postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the underground stems of the ginger plant, which contain high amounts of bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols that help alleviate postnasal drip symptoms by reducing inflammation and congestion.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh ginger root and dry it in the shade for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried ginger into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered ginger with 1000 milliliters of distilled water to create an infusion solution.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth.
Fill #00 gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the filtered ginger infusion and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with post nasal drip because they provide a soothing and protective coating to the mucous membranes lining the nasal passages. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis leaves and flowers and dry them according to proper preservation methods.
Measure 10 grams of dried althaea officinalis and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground althaea officinalis with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a large saucepan and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any solid particles and discard the solids.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the filtered liquid extract and store them in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried cichorium intybus leaves from your local herb store or online supplier.
Grind the cichorium intybus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the ground cichorium intybus with 50 milliliters of boiling distilled water to create an infusion.
Steep the mixture in a glass container at room temperature for 30 minutes before straining and cooling.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the cooled infusion and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain the essential oil of the herb, which has natural expectorant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from a trusted source or purchase them from a reputable supplier.
Grind 250mg of the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered rosmarinus officinalis with 500mg of empty capsule shells and fill capsules to desired size using a capsule filler machine or by hand.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 1 year to maintain potency.
Take 2-3 capsules twice daily with water as needed, starting with a low dose and gradually increasing as necessary to alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for post nasal drip are natural supplements designed to alleviate the discomfort and congestion associated with this condition. These capsules contain a blend of herbs that help to clear mucus, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy respiratory system. The best herbal capsules for post nasal drip include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Sambucus nigra, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Hydrastis canadensis, Ginkgo biloba, Echinacea angustifolia, and Ephedra sinica. If left untreated, post nasal drip can lead to fatigue, sinus infections, and difficulty sleeping, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to post nasal drip that are treated with herbal capsules are sinusitis, bronchitis, and allergies. Herbal capsules containing ingredients such as turmeric, ginger, and boswellia can help alleviate congestion and inflammation in the respiratory tract. Capsules made from herbs like echinacea and elderberry may also boost immunity and reduce symptoms associated with upper respiratory infections. These natural remedies can provide relief without harsh side effects or dependency on prescription medications.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with post nasal drip is a blend of Echinacea, Eucalyptus, and Licorice root. Echinacea boosts the immune system, reducing inflammation and congestion. Eucalyptus helps to thin mucus and ease congestion, while Licorice root soothes the throat and reduces inflammation. These capsules work together to alleviate symptoms of post nasal drip, such as coughing and sinus pressure. They can be taken 2-3 times a day for optimal relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain the essential oils of the eucalyptus plant, which have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. As a result, many people find that taking eucalyptus globulus capsules helps to alleviate the discomfort and frustration associated with this common condition.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This compound helps by thinning mucus and reducing its viscosity, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and post nasal drip.

Catechin: This phenolic compound exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections that can cause post nasal drip by reducing the growth of bacteria and fungi in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are commonly used due to their high eucalyptol content, which helps to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm nasal passages.
Buds: Eucalyptus globulus buds are used due to their rich content of essential oils, including eucalyptol, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote drainage in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a trusted supplier or cultivated source.
Dry the leaves further in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mill or blender for 1 minute.
Mix 200g of the powder with 200g of gelatin and 100g of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with post nasal drip because they have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce congestion and swelling in the nasal passages. The herb's immunomodulatory effects also help boost the body's natural defenses, allowing it to fight off underlying infections that may be contributing to the post nasal drip. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea's expectorant properties help thin and clear mucus, providing relief from the discomfort and congestion associated with post nasal drip. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from post nasal drip symptoms.

Iridoid glycosides: Possesses antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of microorganisms that can lead to infections and subsequent post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of echinacoside, a key compound that helps alleviate congestion and post nasal drip.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the summer months when in full bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Crush the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the powdered plant material with 2 parts of gelatin or cellulose to create a capsule filling.
Fill gelatin or vegetable capsules with the mixture and seal them by hand or using a machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Grind the root into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar.
Mix the powder with 10-20 grams of starch or cellulose powder as filler.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the herbal powder mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain a concentrated dose of Elderberry's active compounds, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried elderberry flowers and berries from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Grind the dried elderberry mixture into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 1:4 ratio of elderberry powder to a carrier agent like tapioca starch or cornstarch in a bowl.
Use a capsule filling machine or a dropper to fill 100 empty vegetable capsules with the mixture.
Label and date the filled capsules and store them in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried zingiber officinale root in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested root in a dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 20 grams of dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix the powdered root with 10 grams of gelatin in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill 50 empty gelatin capsules with the root-gelatin mixture using a powder filling machine or spoon.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain the herb thyme, which has natural decongestant and antimicrobial properties. Dry the fresh leaves in a single layer using a food dehydrator at 40c for 8 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder with 10g quantity.
Mix the powdered thymus vulgaris with 100g of vegetable cellulose and 50g of silicon dioxide.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal using a capsule filling machine with 500mg quantity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain goldenseal, a natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent that reduces congestion and soothes the nasal passages. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of dried hydrastis canadensis root in late summer or early fall from the wild.
Dry the harvested root in a warm dry place for 2-3 weeks or until it reaches 5-8 moisture.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered root with 200-300 milligrams of filler material like cellulose in a bowl.
Fill 100-200 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a small spoon and a clean workspace.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with post nasal drip because they have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for unobstructed drainage and alleviating congestion. Dry the ginkgo biloba material in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 250g of the ginkgo biloba powder with 25g of gelatin and 10g of glycerin.
Fill 500 capsules with 250mg of the mixture each and seal immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia capsules helps with post nasal drip because they contain compounds that stimulate the mucous membranes, promoting a healthier balance of mucus in the nasal passages. Clean and dry the harvested roots for 2-3 days in a warm well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 250 milligrams of a binding agent in a ratio of 2:1.
